text: Ring roller coaster three is the final ring roller coaster exercise in our curriculum at Lahey. It follows the same format and rules as ring roller coaster one and two, but provides a even more difficult course that the rings must travel around the wire.

The surgeon uses the right hand to grab the ring in the anterior portion with a pronated hand position. The hand view in the upper left of the screen shows the da Vinci hand controls being manipulated with the fingertips, maximizing range of motion through the course.
The camera view is kept close and centered in the working field to minimize small collisions on the wire, with frequent adjustments visible to prevent additional collisions.

When experiencing a collision, which could cause a penalty, trying to correct for that is much easier when you're on a more straight or linear path versus on one of the curves or angles of the wire course. You'll see that as we come to this two right angle turns, we have a collision, but again, correct it once we get over that turn.

The surgeon navigates two curves in the wire course using full wrist articulation, resulting in visible collisions between the rings and the wire. These collisions suggest the camera position may need adjustment for better visualization.
Success on this exercise entails using the tips of your fingers on the hand controls, maximizing the full range of motion with the wrist articulation and good camera coordination.
